Ink Wash Literati.text: A man who shows his cards too early is handing others a knife.

Ink Wash Literati.label: Observation
Ink Wash Literati.heading: Two Types at the Dinner Table

left.title: The Newly Promoted
points: Talks about resources, connections, projects
Others nod politely, eyes calculating

right.title: The Other Man
points: Speaks little, asks the right questions
By evening's end, has read everyone's hand

items.title: Shift Center
items.desc: Move out of others' line of sight

Ink Wash Literati.label: Historical Proof
Ink Wash Literati.heading: Han Xin's Humiliation
points: Not glorifying humiliation
He didn't stake his life on a street fight
Winning the small battle is just venting
Winning the big battle earns the right to rise

Ink Wash Literati.label: Strategy
Ink Wash Literati.heading: Turn Restraint Into a Shield
Ink Wash Literati.desc: Be a bit slower, a bit duller, a bit calmer — reduce needless friction.

columns.title: Passive Avoidance
points: Motivation: fear of losing
State: weaker with each retreat
Silence as excuse
Inner feeling: resentment

columns.title: Strategic Restraint
points: Motivation: to win
State: stronger with each concealment
Silence as stored energy
Inner feeling: planning

Ink Wash Literati.label: Core Output
Ink Wash Literati.heading: Hide Four Things · Cultivate Four Powers

steps.title: Conceal Judgment
steps.desc: Don't pick sides in unclear situations

Ink Wash Literati.text: The hidden dragon does not act — not from lack of power, but because the time has not come.
Stay Calm, Stay Hidden, Wait · Let Results Speak

Plant Dye Poetry.title: Clouds Woven, Flora Composed

Plant Dye Poetry.heading: The Most Beautiful Colors
points: The most beautiful colors aren't on a Pantone card
They've always been hidden in mountains, rivers, and plants
2025 Spring Festival Gala visual show · 13 heritage crafts
Yellow-green gradient · butterfly fairy

Plant Dye Poetry.heading: Five Cardinal Colors × Hand Dip Dyeing

left.title: Yellow · Earth
points: Sourced from Huanglu in Shennongjia, Hubei
Represents the Chinese earth

right.title: Blue-Green · Spring
points: Sourced from early spring Malan grass in Jiangnan
Represents endless vitality

Plant Dye Poetry.heading: The Art of Dyeing · Since Ancient Times

events.title: Plant Juices

Plant Dye Poetry.label: Botanical Colors
Plant Dye Poetry.heading: Botanical Colors · Dyeing Nature's Palette
rows: Emperor Huizong: where sky breaks after rain
Liu Yong: mist over a thousand miles
Consort of Li Yu · silk left in dew

columns.title: Silver Red
points: #E49AA5
Light crimson · dawn-touched
Daiyu's window gauze at dawn

columns.title: Flushed
points: Beauty flushed as if drunk
Yang Guifei drunk on wine

columns.title: Longing Gray
points: An inch of longing, an inch of ash
Tea leaves + pomegranate peel

Plant Dye Poetry.heading: Dye Plants · Four Colors from Flora

items.title: Red: Madder · Sappan · Safflower
items.desc: Warm hues from petals and roots

items.title: Yellow-Black: Pagoda Tree · Walnut Hull
items.desc: Natural gifts from buds and shells

steps.title: Dye & Fix

Plant Dye Poetry.label: Cultural Lineage
Plant Dye Poetry.heading: Xue Tao Paper · A Thousand Years of Elegance
Plant Dye Poetry.desc: Tang poet Xue Tao dyed letter paper with hibiscus bark into crimson stationery

Plant Dye Poetry.label: Traditional Chinese Colors
Plant Dye Poetry.desc: From botanical hues born of heaven and earth to silk brilliance passed down a thousand years. Chinese color = visual experience × cultural memory.

Plant Dye Poetry.text: Color is more than vision — it is the bearer of cultural memory.

Imperial Indigo.title: Lin Huiyin: Architect Overshadowed by Literary Fame
Imperial Indigo.subtitle: 70th Anniversary · Rediscovering a Woman's Force in Chinese Architecture

events.title: First Paper
events.desc: On Several Features of Chinese Architecture

Imperial Indigo.label: Practice
Imperial Indigo.heading: Early Architectural Practice After Returning Home

items.title: 1929 Liang Qichao Tombstone
items.desc: First work upon returning from study abroad

items.title: 1932 Peking University Geology Building
items.desc: Earliest introduction of Western modernism to China

left.title: Monument to the People's Heroes
points: Proposed creative revision to the design
Personally designed pedestal ornamentation and wreath reliefs
Based on Tang dynasty style

right.title: Babaoshan Revolutionary Cemetery
points: Main building layout design
Also buried in the space she helped design

Imperial Indigo.label: Pioneer
Imperial Indigo.heading: First Architectural Paper: A Revelation
points: First architectural theory article published by a Chinese scholar
Defined basic features of the timber frame structural system
Rebutted Western misreadings of Chinese architecture
Drew a complete conceptual framework for Chinese architectural history

steps.title: "Architectural Intent" Concept
steps.desc: Convergence of technique, beauty, history, and human feeling

steps.title: Qing Style Construction Manual
steps.desc: Summarized theoretical framework of Chinese architecture

steps.title: Folk Architecture Pioneer
steps.desc: First to propose protection of vernacular buildings

Imperial Indigo.label: Resilience
Imperial Indigo.heading: Architectural Fighter Through War
Imperial Indigo.desc: Surveying ancient buildings in remote valleys — wind, rain, and hardship without retreat.

Imperial Indigo.text: What beauty? I have so much work to do!

Imperial Indigo.label: Legacy
Imperial Indigo.heading: The Career She Wanted to Pursue for Life

columns.title: Disciplinary Foundation
points: Co-founded architecture departments at Northeastern and Tsinghua
Surveyed ancient buildings in remote wilderness

columns.title: Intellectual Pioneer
points: Poured final energy into national emblem and monument
A pioneer in thought

Imperial Indigo.text: Architect Lin Huiyin's Tomb · She proved with her life that she was an architect in every sense

Heritage Brick.title: Lin Huiyin
Heritage Brick.subtitle: Rediscover the Architect, Not the Legend
Heritage Brick.info: 70th Anniversary · Re-examined through work, theory, and action

Heritage Brick.label: Anniversary
Heritage Brick.desc: What gets seen is often not her most important part. What truly supports her historical standing is architectural practice, theoretical construction, and disciplinary foundation.

Heritage Brick.label: Practice
Heritage Brick.heading: From Tombstone to Station: A Clear Architectural Trajectory

events.title: Liang Qichao Tombstone
events.desc: Starting point upon returning from abroad

items.title: Jihai Railway Station
items.desc: Symbolic expression in landmark public architecture

left.title: Monument to the People's Heroes
points: Proposed expanded pedestal and double-tier steps
Ornamentation and scale reflecting traditional architectural vocabulary

right.title: Babaoshan Revolutionary Cemetery
points: Main layout design
She was ultimately buried here too

Heritage Brick.label: Theoretical Foundation
Heritage Brick.heading: In 1932, She Was Already Defining "Chinese Architecture"
points: First systematic discourse on Chinese architecture by a Chinese professional scholar
Rebutted misreadings within Western knowledge frameworks
Proposed key features of the timber frame system
Laid the theoretical skeleton for Chinese architectural history narrative

Heritage Brick.label: Methodology
Heritage Brick.heading: What She Truly Left Was a Method

columns.title: Research Method
points: Survey, fieldwork, and historical analysis combined
Architectural intent, construction manuals

columns.title: Disciplinary Building
points: Participated in Northeastern and Tsinghua architecture departments
Housing survey, vernacular architecture protection

steps.title: Field Survey
steps.desc: Crossing mountains and rivers — not a bystander

steps.title: Professional Mission
steps.desc: "I have so much work to do"

Heritage Brick.text: To rediscover her is not to return from legend to gossip, but from gossip to profession. What she left is not just works, but a language for how China understands its own architecture.
Seven words on the tombstone — already accurate enough.
